title: Flaky Fragrant Egyptian Pastry Flatbread
description: A garlicky, flaky, lacto-vegetarian-friendly Egyptian dish featuring peach juice and ferment, perfect for a brunch mood. The combination of flavors and textures creates a delicious and satisfying treat.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, yeast, and salt.
Gradually add the peach juice, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and roll it out into a thin sheet.
Brush the melted butter and beaten egg over the dough, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the minced garlic over the dough, making sure to stay within the border.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle or rectangle, and press the edges together to seal the pastry.
Place the pastry on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
